items.summary: LIV Golf will run as planned in 2026 according to the league's CEO, as media reports suggest its funding from Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund may soon end.

items.title: Unemployment remains at 4.3pc, ASX closes lower — as it happened
items.summary: The ASX closes lower after the release of ABS labour force data showing the unemployment remains at 4.3 per cent in seasonally adjusted terms. Viva Energy is in a trading halt after a fire at its Geelong oil refinery.

items.title: Farmers selling cattle in record numbers as feed becomes scarce and El Niño forecast
items.summary: More than 500,000 head of cattle have already been sold across New South Wales this year, with Scone seeing its biggest sale in 20 years.

items.title: Australia's Cameron Green' poor IPL season continues in Kolkata Knight Riders' loss to Chennai Super Kings
items.summary: Cameron Green has suffered another challenging outing in the Indian Premier League as he struggles to live up to his tag as the most expensive overseas player.

items.title: States to reveal if they support privatisation of Big Bash League clubs, with possible IPL part-ownership
items.summary: Cricket Australia will know in the coming days if the states are happy to privatise their Big Bash League clubs, a call that may see them become part-owned by Indian Premier League franchises.

items.title: Super Netball round-up: Expansion pushed back and the growing trend to call greats out of retirement
items.summary: Two new teams were expected to join the league in 2027 to make Super Netball a 10-team competition, but slow broadcast negotiations are holding up the process.

items.title: Police thank Nannup community after 87-year-old bushwalker rescued
items.summary: Police say the woman wandered down a rabbit track after becoming disoriented on a weekend bushwalk.

items.title: Business confidence crashes to lowest level since April 2020 — as it happened
items.summary: The outbreak of the war in the Middle East saw business confidence post its second-largest monthly fall on record, a NAB survey found.

items.title: How these queer couples divide the mental load in their household
items.summary: After publishing our story on the four stages of the mental load, we asked to hear from queer couples about how they divide and conquer the cognitive and emotional labour at home.

items.title: Coral Coast tourism hit with mass cancellations after Cyclone Narelle
items.summary: A whale shark boat skipper says one-off payments for tour operators will make a "small difference", as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle's aftermath drags on.

items.title: Police locate hiker who sheltered overnight after missing for 24 hours in Victoria's north-west
items.summary: Police have located a hiker who separated from his group and was missing for more than 24 hours in Victoria's north-west.

items.title: Liberals to offer $5,000 to overseas officers to relocate under plan to recruit 3,000 police
items.summary: The opposition says officers from the UK, Ireland and New Zealand would be offered $5,000 to relocate under a plan to recruit 3,000 new officers.

items.title: Life as a human cannonball.
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Rotting sperm whale carcass forces relocation of Tasmania's largest open water swim
items.summary: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

items.title: Australia vs Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup: Talia Gibson and Kimberly Birrell lose singles
items.summary: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

items.title: ASX 200 and Aussie dollar down, Brent crude and WTI oil up — as it happened
items.summary: The Australian market has finished the trading week on a disappointing note, dropping slightly, despite US stocks rising overnight. Look back on our business blog.

items.title: Construction sector warns of housing delays, mass lay-offs due to Middle East war
items.summary: Contractors, business owners and economists warn that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: A historical orbit, emotional support animals and a tiny outback town
items.summary: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

items.title: Do you ever need to throw out your old bath towels?
items.summary: Expert tips on getting the most out of your favourite bath towels and when it might be time to replace them.

items.title: Regular unleaded petrol to peak in May — as it happened
items.summary: Westpac has drawn a line in the sand and said unleaded petrol prices are projected to peak at $2.46 a litre in late May, with a 26 cents increase when excise returns to normal. Look back at how the trading session unfolded with our specialist business reporters.

items.title: UK man who sexually abused girl has visa reinstated over 'strong ties' to Australia
items.summary: The man's permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld his appeal in part due to his "ill health".

items.title: Severe Cyclone Maila moving again towards Far North Queensland after stalling in Solomon Sea
items.summary: Cyclone Maila has woken up and is heading west, with the category four system still likely to reach Queensland's far north on Monday.

items.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over death of Friends star Matthew Perry
items.summary: Jayvee Sangha admits to running a "stash house" that helped supply the famous Friends star ketamine in the months before his death.

items.title: TV quiz: Match the quote to the classic Australian television show
items.summary: One quote, two possible answers: test your knowledge and take a nostalgic trip down memory lane as you match the quote to the iconic Australian television series.

items.title: What is financial stress and how does it affect our decisions?
items.summary: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

items.title: Childcare crisis hurts families facing two-year wait in Goondiwindi region
items.summary: In the depths of grief, this family wants to grow. But a childcare crisis stands in its way.

items.title: Social media posts educating public about illicit drugs being removed by Meta, Australian health experts say
items.summary: Public health workers are warning lives could be at risk due to social media sites automatically censoring educational posts about illicit drugs circulating in the community.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
items.summary: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

items.title: Man found dead after crossing floodwaters on foot in SA's far north
items.summary: Police have found the body of a man in his 60s who was swept away trying to cross a flooded road on foot in SA's far north over the Easter weekend.

items.title: Tim David stars in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's win over Chennai Super Kings
items.summary: It is a mixed day for the Australians in the Indian Premier League, with Tim David powering RCB to a victory, while Travis Head fails to make an impact in Sunrisers Hyderabad's loss in Lucknow.

items.title: Alleged cocaine smugglers' three-month journey from Panama to Sydney ends in arrest
items.summary: Setting off from Panama just before Christmas, police say the MV Raider started its journey towards Sydney with almost 6 tonnes of cocaine aboard worth nearly $2 billion.

items.title: Interest in novated leases for electric vehicles soaring in Australia amid fuel crisis
items.summary: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering getting an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

items.title: Algal bloom tracks further north up the Yorke Peninsula, sparking concern for stingrays
items.summary: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

items.title: Sam Konstas left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for 2026-27 season
items.summary: Sam Konstas is one of six players left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for the upcoming season.

items.title: NT tourism operators facing hundreds of thousands in damage costs after multiple devastating weather events
items.summary: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

items.title: From screen to symphony: Joff Bush on Bluey’s new orchestral album and music from the hit series
items.summary: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

items.title: Tributes for farming stalwart Ian Burnett and grandson after farming accident
items.summary: Tributes are flowing in for a central Queensland producer who died with his grandson when machinery rolled on a property at the weekend.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Kiera Austin creates period shorts, Kate Moloney collision raises safety questions and Mary Cholhok must lift
items.summary: World Cup-winning Diamond Kiera Austin remembers having to learn to use a tampon earlier than she was ready in order to play netball. Now she's developed a period undershort she hopes will address the alarming rates of Australian teens dropping out of sport.

items.title: Drivers winched to safety after ignoring road closures in WA's flooded Gascoyne following Cyclone Narelle
items.summary: Authorities say motorists are repeatedly ignoring warnings not to drive in floodwaters in WA's north in the wake of Cyclone Narelle, with 20 people having to be rescued.

items.title: More than 200 buildings damaged in Exmouth, residents stranded as airport, roads remain closed
items.summary: Visitors in Exmouth who were stuck there when Cyclone Narelle passed are now trying to find ways out of the town, but that is proving impossible for one grieving family.

items.title: Australia takes 2-0 lead in three-game ODI series against West Indies at Warner Park
items.summary: Australia takes an unassailable 2-0 lead over the West Indies with a cruisy 91-run victory in the second ODI at St Kitts' Warner Park.

items.title: Festival encourages people to see seaweed's 'importance and beauty'
items.summary: South Australia's rugged south-east coast is known for its cold water smashing into limestone cliffs. But beneath the surface is a global hotspot of seaweed biodiversity.

items.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys WA banana crops, Carnarvon growers hit hard
items.summary: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

items.title: In a quest for an affordable mortgage my family tried apartment living
items.summary: In a quest for an affordable mortgage in our location of choice, my family decide to rent an apartment as a trial. Will high-rise living be a realistic alternative to a house as a place to bring up our child?

items.title: How an Irukandji jellyfish the size of a fingernail causes 'unimaginable pain'
items.summary: A tiny jellyfish found in Queensland has venom so powerful it causes feelings of "impending doom".

items.title: Critically endangered hawksbill sea turtle returns to wild months after beach rescue
items.summary: What passers-by thought was a "sandy rock" was in fact a critically endangered turtle far from home.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Mannix starts Mums WhatsApp group, Wallam's revenge and Housby back to her best
items.summary: Emily Mannix talks about her extended absence from the Super Netball league, and why she's now ready to return for the Melbourne Vixens after a "freak accident" meant her daughter Mabel needed emergency care at birth.

items.title: Home building industry facing COVID-like price spikes due to Middle East war
items.summary: From sand to concrete, suppliers are bringing in "emergency fuel levies" as petrol prices soar due to the war in the Middle East. Major companies that supply pipes for plumbing are even warning of shortages.

items.title: How award-winning pianist Andrea Lam learned to let go of perfection
items.summary: She made her debut with Sydney Symphony Orchestra at 13, but don't call Andrea Lam a piano prodigy. The award-winning pianist shares how she's kept kicking career goals without losing her love of the music.

items.title: Who is Anna Lapwood, the content creator bringing organ music to millions on social media
items.summary: Anna Lapwood uses social media to share her love of the organ and favourite music from Bach to Lord of the Rings. Millions follow her and thousands more come to her concerts.

items.title: Petrol price spiral could put mobile early childcare education services at risk
items.summary: Mobile services travelling across regional New South Wales say rising petrol prices are putting early childcare education at risk.

items.title: Queensland's construction worker shortfall to peak at 35,000 ahead of 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games, report warns
items.summary: With new figures laying bare the state of the problem, the construction industry warns cost blow-outs and delays are inevitable unless something is done.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Return of the mums, retired shooter winds back the clock and Kiwis make hot start at Firebirds
items.summary: The world's best goal keeper has sparked an important conversation about postpartum depression in her return to the Super Netball league.

items.title: These musicians stole our hearts on the first season of The Piano, so what are they up to now?
items.summary: In the first season of The Piano, nobody quite knew what to expect. But what happens after you get the chance to play for a national audience? We catch up with some of the pianists to find out how music continues to shape their lives.

items.title: The Bikers Against Child Abuse helping kids reclaim a sense of safety
items.summary: BACA members accompany kids to court, stand guard outside their homes, and provide a sense of safety with burly presences and deeply rumbling motorbikes.

items.title: Fresh twist in case of child molester and former fugitive Charles Batham
items.summary: There been a fresh twist in the long-running saga of child molester Charles Batham, with NSW authorities requesting his transfer from jail interstate to face new charges.

items.title: Shane Wanganeen sentenced for neglect of 7yo son Makai
items.summary: A father who "prolonged his child's suffering" by failing to get him medical attention for 16 hours before his death, has been sentenced to 19 months in prison.

items.title: After 40 years of parenthood incentives Singapore's fertility rate is still falling
items.summary: Singapore is well-known for its social engineering to manage population growth. So why has the island city recorded its lowest-ever fertility rate again?

items.title: Bird deaths linked to fish lost in algal bloom, SA Research and Development Institute says
items.summary: Birds are starving and dying along metropolitan Adelaide beaches, caused by a lack of fish as a result of the algal bloom, the South Australian government says.

items.title: Labor on track to win Nightcliff by-election, taking NT's single Greens seat
items.summary: Labor is on track to win the Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' first and only seat held in the Northern Territory parliament.

items.title: One Nation selects agricultural businessman David Farley to contest Farrer by-election in NSW
items.summary: David Farley, 69, defeats two other candidates in today's preselection battle at the Albury Entertainment Centre.

items.title: Nightcliff by-election: Greens bid to hold Darwin seat challenged by three contenders
items.summary: It started as a historic win for the Greens, but now the minor party is in for a fight to preserve its solitary seat in the Northern Territory Parliament.

items.title: Man charged with manslaughter over death of Jarrad Antonovich in ayahuasca and kambo ceremony
items.summary: Jarrad Antonovich died from a perforated oesophagus after allegedly participating in a ceremony involving South American hallucinogen ayahuasca and the frog poison, kambo.

items.title: The Nightcliff by-election is this weekend. Here is why it's happening and how to vote
items.summary: Residents living in Darwin's northern suburbs of Nightcliff, Rapid Creek and parts of Coconut Grove will be heading to the polls this Saturday to vote in a by-election. It comes after the former member for Nightcliff resigned in February.
